close_reason: Lifetimes are not elidable: multiple input lifetimes require explicit annotation
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:592 'fn json_navigate<'a>(bytes: &'a [u8], path: &[&str]) -> Result<&'a [u8], SecretError>' and 602 'fn json_find_value_b<'a>(bytes: &'a [u8], key: &str) -> Result<&'a [u8], SecretError>' use explicit lifetime annotations.

By the lifetime elision rules: a function with two reference parameters and a reference output can elide *only if the output lifetime can be inferred*. Here we have 'bytes: &'a' and 'path: &[&str]' (or 'key: &str'), producing '&'a [u8]'. The output is borrowed from 'bytes', not from 'path'/'key'. Elision would tie the output to 'bytes' if 'bytes' were the only reference parameter — but multiple reference parameters require the explicit annotation.

Conclusion: the annotations are required. NOT elidable. Filing as a P3 'verify and document' rather than 'fix' — leave the annotations, but consider a comment '// 'a ties output to bytes; path/key lifetimes irrelevant' to make this obvious to future readers.

If a contributor 'cleans up' the lifetime in a future PR thinking it's elidable, they will get a compile error and be confused.
title: json_navigate / json_find_value_b explicit lifetime annotations are likely elidable
